description:
Your role is that of a backup-integrity engineer who has just received a fresh nightly dump.  
  The directory /home/user/backups/daily/ is already populated and contains:

    • data1.txt  
    • data2.txt  
    • data3.txt  
    • expected_sha256.txt   ← pre-generated list of reference checksums

  Management needs an auditable proof that the three text files are still intact.  
  Verify the data *in one single terminal command* and capture **all** command output
  (both STDOUT and STDERR) to the log file:

      /home/user/logs/backup_integrity_check.log

  Output format that the automated grader will check:
    • The log must contain exactly one line for every .txt data file, in this order:
          data1.txt: OK
          data2.txt: OK
          data3.txt: OK
    • No header, footer, or extra lines.
    • Each line is terminated with a single newline character (ASCII 0x0a).

  Hints you may find helpful (do not print these in the log):
    – The reference file already has the correct path-relative filenames inside it.  
    – Redirect both STDOUT and STDERR so that any potential error messages are captured too.  
    – Only one command should appear in your history for this verification step.

  When you are done you should have:
    1. An unchanged /home/user/backups/daily/expected_sha256.txt  
    2. A new file /home/user/logs/backup_integrity_check.log that matches the exact format above.

  No other folders or files should be created or altered.
truth:
Pre-existing file system state before the agent starts:

  1. Directory structure:
       /home/user/backups/daily/
       /home/user/logs/

  2. File hashes and contents:

     2.1 /home/user/backups/daily/data1.txt  
         — file length: 0 bytes (empty file)
         — SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

     2.2 /home/user/backups/daily/data2.txt  
         — file length: 0 bytes (empty file)
         — SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

     2.3 /home/user/backups/daily/data3.txt  
         — file length: 0 bytes (empty file)
         — SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

     2.4 /home/user/backups/daily/expected_sha256.txt  
         (exact byte-for-byte contents; each line ends with \n):
            e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  data1.txt
            e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  data2.txt
            e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  data3.txt

  Expected state after successful execution:

     3.1 /home/user/logs/backup_integrity_check.log exists and contains exactly:
            data1.txt: OK
            data2.txt: OK
            data3.txt: OK
         (Each line ends with a single newline character, and there is
          no trailing blank line after the third line.)

     3.2 None of the original files under /home/user/backups/daily/ have been modified.
